campaigns.title: What makes an AI town feel alive?
campaigns.summary: Show the Rival slice through one visible chain of NPC goals, collisions, and consequences.
campaigns.audience: Players and builders interested in agentic NPCs and browser simulations
campaigns.claim: AliveVille is a browser-playable AI town where goal-driven NPCs create emergent stories.
campaigns.hook: Most AI NPC demos can talk. Can they actually want something?
campaigns.proof: Follow one Rival interaction from goal to action to consequence in the live slice.

campaigns.title: Inside an agentic NPC simulation
campaigns.summary: Use the Director Console to explain the state, goals, and decisions behind one visible NPC action.
campaigns.audience: Game developers and AI simulation researchers
campaigns.claim: The Director Console exposes the state behind AliveVille's NPC behavior.
campaigns.hook: The interesting part is not what the NPC says. It is why it moved.
campaigns.proof: Pair one gameplay moment with the corresponding goal and state in the Director Console.

campaigns.title: A profile people can ask
campaigns.summary: Demonstrate how a Karte profile turns repeated questions about a person into direct, sourced answers.
campaigns.audience: Creators, founders, consultants, and public internet people
campaigns.claim: Karte combines a public profile, links, and an AI answer surface in one shareable page.
campaigns.hook: A link in bio makes visitors hunt. What if they could just ask?
campaigns.proof: Ask one real profile question and show the answer leading back to the relevant public link.

campaigns.title: From scattered links to one useful profile
campaigns.summary: Walk through importing the minimum set of links and context needed to make a Karte profile useful.
campaigns.audience: Independent professionals with fragmented public profiles
campaigns.claim: A useful Karte profile can centralize public links and answer common visitor questions.
campaigns.hook: Your public work is already online. The problem is that nobody knows where to look.
campaigns.proof: Show the before state, the imported links, and the first answer produced from them.

campaigns.title: Tailor evidence, not adjectives
campaigns.summary: Compare a generic resume bullet with a role-specific version that preserves facts and makes the relevant evidence visible.
campaigns.audience: Job seekers who prefer high-quality applications over bulk applications
campaigns.claim: RolePatch creates role-specific application material from a job and the candidate's existing evidence.
campaigns.hook: Resume tailoring should not mean inventing a new person for every job.
campaigns.proof: Show one before-and-after bullet and the job requirement that caused the change.

campaigns.title: One job, one complete apply packet
campaigns.summary: Show how one saved role becomes a tailored resume, company context, interview prep, and a reviewed apply decision.
campaigns.audience: Job seekers managing a selective application pipeline
campaigns.claim: RolePatch keeps tailoring, company research, interview preparation, and application review connected to the same role.
campaigns.hook: A resume file is not an application workflow.
campaigns.proof: Walk through the artifacts attached to one role from save to reviewed packet.

campaigns.title: How one person operates a product fleet
campaigns.summary: Use a real Fleet workflow to show how product state, tasks, deployments, and agent work stay visible across many repositories.
campaigns.audience: Solo builders and small teams operating multiple software products
campaigns.claim: SaaS Maker connects product state, task execution, and fleet operations in one working cockpit.
campaigns.hook: Building product number two is easy. Operating product number twelve is the actual problem.
campaigns.proof: Follow one task from the Fleet overview into its product, code change, and deployment evidence.

campaigns.title: From user feedback to shipped task
campaigns.summary: Demonstrate a feedback item becoming a scoped task, an agent run, and visible release evidence without losing ownership context.
campaigns.audience: Product builders using agents in real delivery workflows
campaigns.claim: SaaS Maker preserves the link between feedback, tasks, execution, and shipped evidence.
campaigns.hook: An agent finishing code is not the same as a product finishing work.
campaigns.proof: Trace one real item through feedback, task state, commit, and deployment health.
